faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Canarsie home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cotton fabrics, silky tubes or cases in closets, adult moths flying around wardrobes, and larvae or cocoons in clothing folds. You may also notice damage to rugs, upholstery, or stored fabrics. Our Canarsie technicians provide free inspections to confirm the presence of clothes moths and assess the extent of the infestation.

faqs.question: What is included in your clothes moth control service for Canarsie residents?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection of closets, storage areas, and affected rooms, identification of moth species and breeding sites, targeted treatment using safe insecticides and growth regulators, removal of infested materials when necessary, and preventive recommendations. We also offer follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination and provide guidance on protecting your garments long-term.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth treatment cost between $550 and $5,000 in Canarsie?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of your Canarsie property, the number of rooms requiring treatment, and whether multiple visits are needed. A minor infestation in one closet starts around $550, while extensive infestations affecting multiple rooms, requiring heat treatment, or needing ongoing monitoring can reach $5,000. We provide free estimates and customize treatment plans to fit your specific situation and budget.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Canarsie home after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets and storage areas, storing woolens and natural fabrics in airtight containers, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, washing or dry-cleaning garments before long-term storage, and maintaining low humidity levels. Our Canarsie team provides detailed prevention strategies during service and offers seasonal maintenance plans to keep your home moth-free year-round.
